title: OrganiGram Holdings Q1 2022 Earnings Summary
text: OrganiGram Holdings Q1 2022 Earnings Summary. Financial Performance: OrganiGram achieved record net revenue of $30.4 million in Q1 fiscal 2022, representing a 57% increase year-over-year. Gross revenue grew 75% to $44.3 million. The company significantly improved profitability metrics, reducing adjusted EBITDA losses from $5.7 million to $1.9 million compared to the prior year. Market Position & Product Success: The company solidified its No. 4 market share position among Canadian licensed producers at 7.5%, up from 4.4% a year prior. The SHRED brand emerged as the company's flagship, claiming the top flower position with consistent market leadership. Operational Improvements: Yield per plant increased dramatically to 129 grams from 86 grams year-over-year, while cost per gram was cut in half. The company harvested approximately 11,600 kilograms of flower in Q1, a 197% increase from prior year levels. Strategic Acquisitions & Investments: OrganiGram completed the $36 million acquisition of Laurentian, a Quebec-based producer, expanding the premium portfolio. The company also increased its investment in Hyasynth Biologicals to $10 million. Forward Guidance: Management advanced its profitability timeline, now targeting positive adjusted EBITDA by Q3 fiscal 2022, accelerated by the Laurentian acquisition and capacity expansions.
